literal: begun, set in motion; past passive participle of pra- + ā- + √rabh 'to begin'
invariant_definition: The portion of *karma* that has begun to fructify in the present life — distinct from *sañcita* (accumulated, not yet active) and *āgāmī* (currently being produced). All Vedāntic schools accept this three-fold *karma* division. The *prārabdha* is what the *jīvanmukta* (in Advaita) lives out: even after *brahma-jñāna* the body persists until *prārabdha-karma* is exhausted. Schools that reject *jīvanmukti* (Dvaita) hold that *brahma-jñāna* / *aparokṣa-jñāna* itself awaits the exhaustion of *prārabdha*.

per_school.definition: *Prārabdha-karma* is what sustains the *jīvanmukta*'s body after *avidyā*-removal. *Sañcita* and *āgāmī* are dissolved by *brahma-jñāna*; *prārabdha* runs its course.
*Prārabdha* must also be exhausted; *mokṣa* (in V) or *aparokṣa-jñāna* (in D) does not exempt the soul from working through *prārabdha* until the body's natural end.
